title: Rate limiting middleware
description:
## What
Implement per-IP and per-OAuth-token rate limiting using an in-process token bucket (no Redis, no sidecar). Configurable limits in `config.toml` (e.g., `[rate_limit] api_per_ip = 300` per 5 minutes, `api_per_token = 300` per 5 minutes). Return HTTP 429 with `Retry-After` header when limit is exceeded. Include Mastodon rate limit headers on all API responses: `X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`, `X-RateLimit-Reset` (RFC 3339 timestamp of window reset).

## Why
Rate limit headers are checked by some clients (Ivory, Elk) to display "slow down" UI. Returning 429 correctly prevents clients from hammering the server. The `X-RateLimit-*` headers must match mastodon.social's shape or clients won't parse them.

## Agent team pattern
- Spawn a Research subagent to: capture mastodon.social's exact `X-RateLimit-*` header values and format (including the `X-RateLimit-Reset` timestamp format) via curl; read the Tower middleware docs for implementing rate limiting as axum middleware; evaluate whether `governor` crate (token bucket) fits the need without pulling in Redis.
- Spawn an Implementation subagent to: add a Tower middleware layer using `governor` crate (or a minimal hand-rolled token bucket) keyed by IP + token; add `X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`, `X-RateLimit-Reset` to all `/api/` responses; return 429 with `{"error": "Too many requests"}` and `Retry-After` header when limit exceeded; add config keys to `config.toml`.
- Spawn a Test subagent to: write integration tests that send N+1 requests and assert the N+1th returns 429 with correct headers; assert that `X-RateLimit-Remaining` decrements on each request; assert `Retry-After` is a positive integer.

## Test discipline
- Independent oracle: mastodon.social rate limit header shapes captured via curl (header names, value formats, `X-RateLimit-Reset` as RFC 3339 string).
- Test approach: integration tests with configurable low limit (e.g., 3 requests per window) to make exhaustion testable without thousands of requests.

## Defensive programming checklist
- No `.unwrap()` in handler paths.
- Rate limit state must be in-process only — no persistence, resets on restart (acceptable for solo-admin use case).
- IP extraction must handle `X-Forwarded-For` (set by reverse proxy) — use the leftmost IP in the chain.
- `X-RateLimit-Reset` must be an RFC 3339 UTC timestamp string matching mastodon.social's format exactly.
- 429 response body must be `{"error": "Too many requests"}` — not HTML, not a Rust panic trace.
- Federation inbox endpoint must have a separate (higher) rate limit or be exempt — follow delivery from mastodon.social must not be rate-limited to the point of rejecting legitimate federation traffic.
